text: Does the Gaussian density packet N(x,0)=2 exp(-(x+5)^2/2.25), phi_x=0.3, u=0.3N on M_cs in B-NLS (focusing kappa=1, user-sign quantum pressure) shed bright solitons (>=2 peaks, amp>=1) by T=6 with mass-drift<5% and boundedness?

method: Fourier pseudospectral on (u,N,phi) + explicit RK4; no dealiasing; no operator splitting; sqrt(N) floor 1e-10
bank_use_rationale: NoKB; simplest meaningful baseline per progressive-complexity discipline.

diagnostics.interp: (sqrt N)_xx / (2 sqrt N) is numerically singular in the Gaussian tails where N underflows to ~1e-77. FFT of sqrt(N) gives non-zero second derivative ~O(1e-2) at the tails which, divided by sqrt(N) ~ 1e-5 (after floor 1e-10), produces Q ~ O(20). This Q drives phi_p in the tails which then aliases back into the bulk via spectral phi_x^2 nonlinearity, causing blow-up at step ~4.

rationale: E1 baseline fails due to ill-defined Q at N->0. Need single-component regularization. Cannot use standard Madelung-Psi since user's +Q sign convention is incompatible (W in Psi eqn would have to depend on Q). Cleanest single change: regularize Q with a small background eps^2 in sqrt -> use sqrt(N+eps^2) for the quantum-pressure denominator and numerator. Equivalent to adding O(eps) vacuum density only in the Q kernel (does not affect N or u dynamics, just stabilizes Q).

method: Fourier pseudospectral on (u, N, phi_p); explicit RK4; quantum-pressure regularization Q = (sqrt(N+eps^2))_xx / (2 sqrt(N+eps^2)) with eps^2=1e-4; no dealiasing, no operator splitting
bank_use_rationale: NoKB; one-component change over E1 = regularize Q via small background in the sqrt argument.

diagnostics.interp: Quantum-pressure regularization helped (Q from 21.7 -> 1.7 at t=0) but did not address the underlying aliasing-driven numerical instability. minN grows ~25x per step (geometric high-k mode growth). At step 7, max|phi_p_x| jumped 13x (from 0.0054 to 0.0675), indicating a high-frequency mode running away through cubic spectral aliasing in the (phi_x^2, u phi_x) products of phi_t. Linear analysis around N_0=2 with V_0=0.3 gives omega^2 > 0 (modulationally stable), so this is purely numerical.

rationale: E2 still blows up via spectral aliasing. Standard single-component fix for spectral instability with quadratic+cubic products: 2/3-rule dealiasing of all nonlinear products. This keeps method (Fourier+RK4+Q-reg), adds one component (dealias mask).

method: E2 + 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear product (u*m, u*N, phi_x*N, phi_x^2, u*phi_x, sqrt(N+eps2)). Also reduce dt to 1e-4 to help stability.
bank_use_rationale: NoKB; one-component upgrade over E2 = 2/3 dealias (with dt halved as a supporting safety margin, still within the same method family).
